expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly restates and addresses the case focus: "Case focus: ‘context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting.’" It ties Phase 1 to preserving those signals: "the snapshot contract supports this by ensuring feature requirements are captured as evidence artifacts under `context/` prior to any drafting (which begins at Phase 4a)." It also enumerates the specific banding/style constraints from the fixture (rows-only banding, no color formatting, no column banding, no header-based banding color) as the context to be preserved.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output is framed as a Phase 1 contract evaluation and stays within Phase 1 scope: "Verdict (phase1, advisory)" and "Primary phase under test: phase1". It references Phase 1 outputs/validations (spawn manifest, evidence collection, non-defect routing) rather than later-phase scenario drafting: "Output: `phase1_spawn_manifest.json`" and "scenario drafting doesn’t start until Phase 4a". It also notes missing Phase 1 runtime artifacts without attempting Phase 4+ work.
